name: schema
context: Schema checks - required fields, URI syntax, content types, and allowed parent relationships
info: This sub-skill covers the hard edges of Dojo manifests: required fields, enum values, URI shape, tag syntax, hierarchy rules like `sub` requiring a `skill` parent, and reference integrity checks that make section-target links and wiki-links safe to follow.
triggers: why is my content type invalid
body:
# Schema Validation

Schema validation checks the hard edges: required fields, URI format, tag syntax, allowed `content_type` values, and the parent and type constraints described in [[dojo/spec#hierarchy]].

The live runtime uses `node.json` for canonical nodes and still accepts legacy `skill.json` manifests. Validate the manifest content, not the filename.

sections.title: Required fields
sections.body: Start with identity (`name`, `version`, `uri`, `type`) and decision text (`context`, `info`), then confirm `tags` are present and properly shaped.

sections.title: Parent rules
sections.body: `ecosystem` is root-only, `sub` must have a `skill` parent, and other node types may live under any non-`sub` parent. Bad parent relationships are often the reason a node looks correct on disk but wrong in the runtime tree.

sections.title: Reference integrity
sections.body: Structural validation should also verify that graph references can actually be followed. Explicit `links`, `depends`, and `related` edges should resolve to real nodes, and section-target references like `dojo/spec#manifest-core` should resolve to real sections instead of dead anchors. The same applies to wiki-links inside `info`, `body`, and section text.

links.context: Knowledge fields whose references should still validate structurally
Definitions for types like skill, standard, context, and sub
Checklist to run after hard validation passes

related.note: This sub-skill applies the formal schema and parent rules

author: dojo-community
